events.agenda: 5:30pm Check-in and networking; 6:00pm Presentations; networking until 7:30pm.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, but presentations can cover broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: Excella, 2300 Wilson Blvd Suite 600, Arlington, VA
events.raw_text:

"Scalable Model Generation with Jinja and Macros" - Vatsal Sanghavi (Senior Analytics Engineer, Datadog)
"Streamlining Development for Strive Health Data Analytics" - Matthew Temba (Chief Technology Officer, Strive Health)

Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, but presentations can cover broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: Fundrise, 11 Dupont Cir NW, Washington, DC
events.raw_text:
Wed, Dec 6, 2023, 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM EST

Ramiz Bozai (Solutions Architect at dbt Labs) will show how the dbt Semantic Layer enables centralized metric definitions alongside dbt models, for consistency in downstream BI reporting.
Chris Brauckmuller (Chief Strategy Officer at Fundrise) will explain how Fundrise uses dbt to run their real estate investment portfolio.

talks.description: How the dbt Semantic Layer enables centralized metric definitions alongside dbt models, for consistency in downstream BI reporting.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Ramiz Bozai (Solutions Architect at dbt Labs) will show how the dbt Semantic Layer enables centralized metric definitions alongside dbt models, for consistency in downstream BI reporting.
talks.title: dbt Semantic Layer for centralized metric definitions

talks.description: How Fundrise uses dbt to run their real estate investment portfolio.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Chris Brauckmuller (Chief Strategy Officer at Fundrise) will explain how Fundrise uses dbt to run their real estate investment portfolio.
talks.title: How Fundrise uses dbt to run their real estate investment portfolio

From Zero to Hero with dbt. Emily will talk through her journey with dbt from first contact to making dbt her favourite data wrangling tool.

additional_metadata.venue_host: Caboose Brewing Co & Tavern, Tysons, VA
additional_metadata.note: Informal casual get-together, not a formal meetup.

events.agenda: Informal gathering to discuss data topics, including work on using public and private datasets.
events.event_description: A casual, informal get-together to talk data, not a formal Meetup.
events.event_name: Washington DC - Casual Get Together - May 11th
events.location: Caboose Brewing Co & Tavern, 520 Mill St NE, Vienna, VA
events.raw_text:
Thu, May 11, 2023 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

This is not a formal Meetup, just a casual opportunity to talk data. James and I have been doing some interesting work around how to make use of data sourced from Public and Private data sets.

events.agenda: 17:30-18:00 Check in/Registration; 18:00-18:05 Welcome Remarks; 18:05-18:35 Presentation; 18:35-18:45 Q&A; 18:45-18:50 Closing Remarks; 18:50-19:30 Networking reception.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: NuAxis Innovations, 8605 Westwood Center Dr #300, Vienna, VA
events.raw_text:
Wed, Mar 15, 2023, 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM EDT

The power of many eyeballs leveraging jinja2 and dbt to supercharge your confidence in your data. James brings a tale from the field using dbt macros to do large scale validation of data sets.

talks.description: Using dbt macros to do large-scale validation of datasets, looking at data holistically instead of only spot checking.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Description: James brings a tale from the field using dbt macros to do large scale validation of data sets. He talks about the need to look at all of the data holistically instead of only spot checking.
talks.title: The power of many eyeballs leveraging jinja2 and dbt to supercharge your confidence in your data
